title: phaseolus vulgaris var. turcicus
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. turcicus, also known as Turkey bean or Turkish kidney bean, is a variety of the common bean (Phaseolus vulgaris) native to the Americas but cultivated in various parts of the world. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities, making it useful in treating conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Phaseolus vulgaris var. turcicus include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve blood sugar control, and provide essential nutrients like protein, fiber, and minerals. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
